checks: Caption-VO mismatch (highest priority after split typos): go through EVERY caption line word by word against the transcript — from the FIRST frame to the LAST. Do not focus only on the opening seconds. Flag any line where even a single word differs — wrong word, missing word, extra word, wrong tense, wrong punctuation. Do not stop at the first mismatch — scan the entire video equally. EXCEPTION: for specialized vocabulary (botanical names, ingredient names, brand names, product names, technical terms), Whisper commonly mishears. Do NOT flag as a mismatch unless you can visually confirm from the caption itself that the word is wrong — trust the caption text for specialized terms.

Duplicate captions visible on screen at the same time — two separate caption layers both showing text simultaneously. Look carefully at every frame: if you see two lines of caption text where one appears to be an overlay on top of another, or the same line appears in two different positions, flag it.
Sentence starts without a capital letter (flag only if it is the start of a new sentence, not a mid-sentence continuation)

Wrong sentence splitting — CRITICAL: even if punctuation is not shown on screen, caption lines must respect sentence and clause boundaries. Flag any line that: (1) merges two separate sentences into one caption, (2) breaks mid-phrase where a natural pause or comma should exist, or (3) separates words that belong together (e.g. 'style –' then 'quiz' when 'style quiz' is a compound phrase). Use the transcript to identify where sentences naturally end. Example of bad split: 'My monthly ThreadBeast box – just arrived you – take a quick style – quiz' — this breaks across three separate sentences with no respect for sentence boundaries.

Caption split typo (highest priority): as captions change frame to frame, check if the LAST WORD of the old caption matches or forms the START of the first word in the new caption — if yes, the word was duplicated across the split. This includes hyphenated words: '...other anti' then 'anti-redness products' = 'anti' duplicated. STRICT RULE: only flag words that are 4 or more characters long. NEVER flag 'and', 'the', 'but', 'for', 'at', 'in', 'on', 'is', 'it', 'or', 'to', 'a', 'an' — these 1-3 character words appear naturally at sentence boundaries and are NOT split typos regardless of position. Flag every confirmed instance with its timestamp.
Captions out of sync: footage changes while captions still show the previous line, or captions jump ahead of the footage

Typo in caption text: a word on screen is misspelled or clearly wrong — cross-reference against the transcript to confirm the correct word. Flag the exact wrong word, the timestamp, and what it should say.

Captions outside safe area: check that caption text is not cut off at the frame edges or positioned so close to the bottom/sides that it would be cropped on standard displays. Flag any caption that appears partially off-screen or dangerously close to the edge.

Missing disclaimer: if the video makes health claims, financial claims, results claims, or promotes a regulated product, check whether a disclaimer or legal text is visible on screen at any point. Flag if no disclaimer is found when one would be expected based on the content.

Incorrect pronunciation: review the transcript for words that appear to have been mispronounced by the talent — especially brand names, medical terms, product names, or industry-specific words. A mispronounced word often shows up in the transcript as an unexpected or phonetically similar substitute (e.g., a brand name transcribed differently from how it appears in captions). If context is provided with the correct pronunciation or spelling, cross-reference it. Flag the timestamp, the word as heard, and what it should be.
do_not_flag: Lowercase captions that are mid-sentence continuations
Anything you cannot confirm from the frames or transcript provided
